results.scenario: client-overview: opened Vandelay Industries, verified the overview exposes the primary contact and contracts, then verified tickets/assets/contracts/primary-contact detail are reachable from the client context
asset-ticket-link: opened T-9, confirmed VAN-APP01 is visible and clickable from the ticket, then confirmed the asset Alerts & Tickets tab links back to T-9
invoice-list/invoice-detail: opened invoice list, status-filtered posted invoices, and verified INV-2026103 client/status/line totals on detail

tix-create-manual: created T-102 for Vandelay Industries with subject QA create manual ticket 20260706105139, P2 priority, and a Markdown description; verified the saved detail and global queue search

notes: No assigned tester bead or authenticated mail assignment was present at run start, so this was a focused autonomous scenario slice.

The client tickets tab first page did not show new T-102 because Vandelay has 61 tickets and the default paginated/sorted view starts with older high-priority rows; global queue search did find T-102. I did not file this as a bug.
The asset-ticket pass reproduced the already-open asset-badge console TypeError. Existing open finding psa-txh4 covers it, so no duplicate was filed.
Billing detail still lacks a standalone mark-paid action for posted INV-2026103, but existing open finding psa-8yhp covers that gap, so no duplicate was filed.
The broad text click on VAN-APP01 initially hit an already-known stale absolute Recent link to 127.0.0.1; existing open finding psa-1xlr covers Recent links storing host-qualified URLs.
